en.name: Boosting the financing of innovation for green growth sectors through innovative clusters services in the MED area
en.description:
Fostering economic growth and development, while ensuring that natural assets continue to provide the resources and environmental services on which prosperity relies, is no more a choice but an imperative for the economic and social future wellbeing of people. Although green transition will not take off without financing and assure the financing of innovation in green sectors has become a common concern for all MED countries and regions. To answer this challenge, a pool of regions, clusters, business support organisations, knowledge providers and development agencies joined in the finMED project, whose aim is to boost the financing of innovation in green growth sectors through improved delivery of policies and strategies and the introduction of innovative cluster services. 
The project delivers concrete outputs and results in three fields:
1- Public policies delivery, by developing and adopting new concepts, principle and practices to finance green growth strategies.
2- Innovation and business environment, by improving interaction and collaboration (at regional and transnational level) among involved stakeholders and bridging the gap among enterprises and financers.
3- Green sectors capacities, by stimulating and actively supporting their adaptability to the most impellent financing challenges.
finMED also develops a proper specific position on the future of green sectors innovation financing, supporting the policy debate for 2021-2028 programming period in MED and EU area.
en.expected_results: 1 Increased understanding, knowledge and capacities related to financing innovation in green sectors by public and private actors in the MED area : The project produces the following concrete changes: -Cluster and business support organisations trained and skilled to provide support service to their members for facilitating access to finance for innovation -Regional public authorities able to make use of differentiated financial solutions to implement green growth policies. And delivers the following tangible and durable benefits to target groups: -Cluster and b.s. organisations: Support service (contents and format) to be provided to companies (esp. SMEs) to support access to finance for innovation -Regional public authorities: tool for mentoring and capacity building will deliver to regional public authorities new skills and competences to open up new opportunities and introduce/reinforce the use of adaptive financial solutions and mechanisms to implement green growth regional policies. Contribution to programme result indicator: the listed changes contribute to the programme result indicator by increasing the number of operational instruments to favour innovation in SMEs (output indicator). Related main deliverables: Tool for capacity building of public authorities (final in D5.5.1), Support service to be provided to companies by clusters and b.s. organisations to support access to finance (final in output 1, WP5).1 Increased understanding, knowledge and capacities related to financing innovation in green sectors by public and private actors in the MED area : The project produces the following concrete changes: -Cluster and business support organisations trained and skilled to provide support service to their members for facilitating access to finance for innovation -Regional public authorities able to make use of differentiated financial solutions to implement green growth policies. And delivers the following tangible and durable benefits to target groups: -Cluster and b.s. organisations: Support service (contents and format) to be provided to companies (esp. SMEs) to support access to finance for innovation -Regional public authorities: tool for mentoring and capacity building will deliver to regional public authorities new skills and competences to open up new opportunities and introduce/reinforce the use of adaptive financial solutions and mechanisms to implement green growth regional policies. Contribution to programme result indicator: the listed changes contribute to the programme result indicator by increasing the number of operational instruments to favour innovation in SMEs (output indicator). Related main deliverables: Tool for capacity building of public authorities (final in D5.5.1), Support service to be provided to companies by clusters and b.s. organisations to support access to finance (final in output 1, WP5).2 Increased access to finance for innovation in green sectors in the MED area : Project produces the following concrete changes: -Cluster and b.s. organisations becoming referent point for issues related to access to finance for innovation for their members and interlocutors towards financial institutions/financers -Regional public authorities increasing availability, accessibility and harmonisation of funds for innovation projects in green sectors through the use of adaptive financial solutions.Tangible and durable benefits to target groups:-Clusters, b.s. organisations: Opportunities to support companies (esp. SMEs) in overcoming financing gaps between investors and entrepreneurs and additional opportunities for SMEs to receive support for financing innovation projects -Public authorities: individual and organisational learning for regional public authorities officials and servants on financial solutions and implementation options to support innovation in green sectors. Contribution to programme result indicator: the listed changes contribute to the programme result indicator by increasing the number of operational instruments to favour innovation in SMEs (output indicator)and by increasing the number of transnational innovation clusters supported (output indicator).Related main deliverables: Testing method and evaluation method of cluster service for supporting access to finance for SMEs, Business2finance meetings, Exchange of information, Transferring of the improved and finalized capacity building tool for public authorities (in WP4 and WP5).3 Reinforced, empowered and more coordinated clusters, business support org. and public authorities in their effort of assuring access to finance in gre : Project produces the following concrete changes: -Cluster organisations increasing their international profile and rise the standards of the services provided to their members (esp. regarding access to finance for innovation) through peer learning and the establishment of permanent linkages with EU financers (EIB, EU wide financial institutions, etc.) -Regional public authorities actively assuring an appropriate level of financing of innovation green sectors, by including the access to finance in their political agendas and facilitating linkages among financers and green sectors market actors.Tangible and durable benefits to target groups:-the transantional network supports MED clusters and b.s.organisations in upgrading their aptitudes toward innovation financing and improve their international/transnational profile (exp. toward financers)-the establishment of finMED network among different actors in the green sectors innovation chain and financers delivers to regional public authorities, clusters and b.s. organisations, SMEs the opportunity to join their common efforts in assuring a proper level of financing to innovation for green sectors.Contribution to programme result indicator: the listed changes contribute to the programme result indicator by increasing the number of transnational innovation clusters supported. Related main deliverables: Policy Procedures for the delivery of green growth policies in the involved project regions (through A6.2, A6.3 and final D6.4.1), high level conference and finMED network (in A6.5).
en.achievements:
During the 6th RP finMED partnership had to carry out several challenging activities, while at the same time still addressing the short/medium term consequences of Covid19 pandemic on each own institution/organisation and regional/national contexts.RP6 included 3 ambitious tasks of the Transferring module, concluding the overall transfer process of the finMED tools: Capacity Building Tool for Public Authorities, Support Service Tool for Clusters and Business Support Organisations and MASDE tool. In the Transferring module, finMED pursued a more active role of clusters and business support organizations, from one side, and public authorities on the other, in assuring access to finance for innovation in green sectors and reinforced linkages among innovation actors and financers. FinMED tools had been subjected to real life “stress-test”, by determining the ability of delivered tools and methodologies to deal with the real economic and financial contexts (through Testing Module and then in A

5.2 and A

5.3). With the finalization of the Support Service Tool in A

5.4 and the Piloting of MASDE tool in A

5.7 (in Valencia Region), done in RP6, finMED is now able to offer concrete, operational and ready-to-use solutions for clusters and BSOs. These solutions can enable them to become referent points for all issues related to access to finance for innovation for their members, associates and beneficiaries; as well to propose themselves as interlocutors towards financial institutions and financers, helping SMEs in particular in accessing information and obtaining targeted support. With the finalized version of the Capacity Building Tool, delivered by A 

5.5, finMED sustained public authorities in increasing the accessibility and harmonization of funds for innovation in green sectors through the use of appropriate financial solutions. RP6 also showed an increased engagement of PPs in communication activities thanks to the enforcement of communication team by the Lead Partner and stronger lead by WP2 leader. More attention has been paid to the project website, to a more attractive visual identity, to the publication of news and the sharing of events in social media. Regarding Project Management, LP adopted all necessary measures to support PPs in this difficult situation, intensifying bilateral communications and providing ad hoc support. The collaboration among PPs as well improved decisively, as more interaction and common activities took place in virtual mode.
en.expected_outputs: Enhanced transnational clusters' portfolio of activities with innovative professional service to facilitate access to finance for innovation,Support service (contents and format) to be provided by clusters and business support organisations to their members (esp. SMEs) to support access to finance for innovation,Policy Procedures for the delivery of green growth policies in the involved project regions
en.delivered_outputs: Number of operational instruments to favour innovation of SMEs, Number of enterprises receiving grants, Number of enterprises receiving non-financial support, Number of transnational innovation clusters supported, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 1,

fr.name: Renforcer le financement de l'innovation pour les secteurs de la croissance verte par le biais de services de clusters innovants dans l’espace MED
fr.description:
Supporter la croissance économique et le développement, et aussi garantir que le patrimoine naturel continue à assurer les ressources et les services environnementaux sur lesquels repose la prospérité, n'est plus un choix mais bien un impératif pour le bien-être économique et social des personnes. Toutefois la transition verte ne décolle pas sans financement et assurer le financement de l'innovation dans les secteurs verts est devenue une préoccupation commune pour tous les Pays et régions MED. Pour répondre à ce défi, les partenaires de finMED s’est joint avec l'objectif de renforcer le financement de l'innovation dans les secteurs de croissance verte grâce à l'amélioration de la mise en œuvre des politiques et des stratégies et de l'introduction des services de cluster innovants. 
Le projet livres des résultats concrets dans trois domaines: 
1 - Mise en œuvre des politiques publiques, en développant et en adoptant de nouveaux concepts, principes et pratiques.
2- Innovation et milieu économique, en améliorant l'interaction et la collaboration (au niveau régional et transnational) entre les acteurs concernés et en comblant l’écart entre les entreprises et les financeurs.
3- Capacités des secteurs verts, en stimulant et en soutenant activement leur adaptabilité aux défis de financement. 
FinMED soutient aussi le débat politique pour la période de programmation 2021-2028, développant une position spécifique sur l'avenir du financement de l'innovation dans les secteurs verts.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d’instruments opérationnels en faveur de l’innovation des PME, Nombre d'entreprises béneficiant de subventions, Nombre d'entreprises recevant un soutien non-financier, Nombre de clusters d'innovation transnationaux soutenus, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 1,

en.name: Preserving biodiversity from plastics in Mediterranean Marine Protected Areas
en.description: The Mediterranean Sea is one of the areas most affected by Marine Litter (ML) in the world. The impacts of ML on marine biota within Marine Protected Areas (MPAs), including endangered species, remain poorly addressed, however it is clear that prevention and mitigation measures are urgently needed. The overall objective of the PlasticBusters MPAs project is to contribute to maintaining biodiversity and preserving natural ecosystems in pelagic and coastal MPAs, by defining and implementing a harmonized approach against ML. The project entails actions that address the whole management cycle of ML, from monitoring and assessment to prevention and mitigation, as well as actions to strengthen networking between and among pelagic and coastal MPAs located in Italy, France, Spain, Croatia, Albania and Greece. This is the first project at basin scale where EU and IPA countries join forces to tackle ML via a coordinated approach in order to: i) diagnose ML impacts on biodiversity in MPAs, including the identification of "ML hotspots"; ii) define and test tailor-made ML surveillance, prevention and mitigation measures in MPAs; iii) develop a common framework of ML actions for Interreg Med regions towards the conservation of biodiversity in Med MPAs. A joint governance plan will be defined through a participatory approach and the concretized commitment of Med MPAs (MoUs) to implement it will result in an increased surface area of habitats supported to attain a better conservation status.
en.expected_results: 1 Quantitative and qualitative diagnosis of the impacts of marine litter in Med MPAs and on marine biodiversity : In order to contribute to the increase of MPAs meeting their conservation goals and objectives, via the reduction of the anthropogenic impacts of ML it is essential to diagnose the extent and nature of the impact (baseline values of pressure and risk related to ML). This will be achieved by producing: - A comprehensive assessment of the status amounts, composition and impacts on marine species of ML in pelagic and coastal MPAs.- GIS ML data to be shared with existing databases (e.g. the IPA-Adriatic DeFishGear GIS database, the EEA’s Marine Litter Watch App database or the EMODnet.- A hotspot analysis/mapping of ML accumulation areas, based on ocean currents and convergence areas and state-of-the-art modelling and satellite-based tools to support the implementation of targeted marine litter management measures in the most affected sites in the MPAs.- The characterization of marine litter, micro- and macro-plastics and assessment of their abundance in the oceanographic structures (convergence areas) identified by the model and identification ML sources in order to design targeted prevention mitigation actions in the MAPs.- The establishment of the impact of ML on biota, including target endangered and sentinel species (cetaceans, sea turtles, large pelagic fish, sea birds, and other sentinel species) according to the trophic level and feeding habits. To define the occurrence and typology of ML in order to design specific mitigation actions.1 Quantitative and qualitative diagnosis of the impacts of marine litter in Med MPAs and on marine biodiversity : In order to contribute to the increase of MPAs meeting their conservation goals and objectives, via the reduction of the anthropogenic impacts of ML it is essential to diagnose the extent and nature of the impact (baseline values of pressure and risk related to ML). This will be achieved by producing: - A comprehensive assessment of the status amounts, composition and impacts on marine species of ML in pelagic and coastal MPAs.- GIS ML data to be shared with existing databases (e.g. the IPA-Adriatic DeFishGear GIS database, the EEA’s Marine Litter Watch App database or the EMODnet.- A hotspot analysis/mapping of ML accumulation areas, based on ocean currents and convergence areas and state-of-the-art modelling and satellite-based tools to support the implementation of targeted marine litter management measures in the most affected sites in the MPAs.- The characterization of marine litter, micro- and macro-plastics and assessment of their abundance in the oceanographic structures (convergence areas) identified by the model and identification ML sources in order to design targeted prevention mitigation actions in the MAPs.- The establishment of the impact of ML on biota, including target endangered and sentinel species (cetaceans, sea turtles, large pelagic fish, sea birds, and other sentinel species) according to the trophic level and feeding habits. To define the occurrence and typology of ML in order to design specific mitigation actions.2 Enhanced protection of biodiversity and ecosystems in pelagic and coastal MPAs through targeted prevention and mitigation actions : Based on the diagnostic analysis of the extent and nature of the impact of ML, tailored-made measures will be defined and implemented to reduce the ML pressure and associated risks in the 47% (pelagic and coastal 9256130 ha) of Med SPAMI and MAPs via: - The production of a set of comprehensive recommendations for decision making purposes, developed on the basis of sound scientific evidence and findings, while also capitalizing on the previous projects (Interreg MED ACT4LITTER, IPA-Adriatic DeFishGear, CLEANSEA, etc.), to facilitate effective policymaking at local, regional and national levels with regards to the prevention, reduction and removal of ML in Med MPAs.- The implementation of a series of demo projects to prevent and mitigate the impacts of ML in hotspot areas, through concrete showcases of measures like fishing for litter, targeted removal of ghost nets, setting up deposit schemes for beverage packaging, single-use plastic bags reduction, etc. - Strengthening the implementation of the key EU and UNEP/MAP related legislative frameworks, national policies and international agreements and developing the enabling environment for the replication of measures towards the reduction of plastic debris input and its effects on a wide network of Med MPAs.- Knowledge transfer and capitalization actions in 6 Mediterranean countries with the aim to promote the uptake of the project results and the concrete commitment of stakeholders towards implementing the joint governance plan.3 Accelerated knowledge transfer to Med MPAs and scaling-up of best practices to tackle ML through enhanced institutional uptake of project results : The project will improve the share of protected areas which meet their conservation goals concerning marine litter via: - Strengthening networking and cooperation between and among pelagic and coastal MPAs and promoting a joint governance scheme against ML.- Catalysing, for decision making purposes, exchange of information, experiences and know-how related to the implementation of targeted prevention and mitigation measures at regional, transnational level, national and local levels.- Capacity building and knowledge transfer to adopt and improve protection measures against ML in order to maintain the biodiversity of coastal and marine ecosystems in the Mediterranean Sea.- Building synergies and capitalizing upon relevant initiatives and projects such as the INTERREG MED ACT4LITTER project, the IPA-Adriatic DeFishGear project, the CLEANSEA project, etc.- Transferring effectively the PlasticBusters MPAs main outputs to a wider range of MED users and territories, generating added value after the end of the project.- Mainstreaming the institutional uptake of the project achievements at local, national and regional level towards achieving good environmental status of marine waters, in particular in MPAs through capitalization events, policy recommendations and lobbying activities.

fr.name: Plastic Busters: préservation de la biodiversité contre les plastiques dans les Aires Marines Protégées Méditerranéennes
fr.description: La Méditerranée est une zone parmi les plus touchées du monde par les déchets marin. À ce jour, les impacts sur les organismes marins habitant des aires marines protégées (AMPs), notamment cétacés et tortues marines, sont mal étudiés, alors que des mesures préventives de réduction sont urgentes. L'objectif global du projet MPA-PlasticBusters est de contribuer au maintien de la biodiversité et préserver les écosystèmes naturels dans les AMPs pélagiques et côtières, par une approche harmonisée contre les déchets marins. Ce projet intégré implique des actions concernant l'ensemble du cycle des déchets en mer relatif aux pressions et leurs impacts ainsi que la mise en réseau des AMPs situées en Italie, France, Espagne, Croatie, Albanie et Grèce. Il s'agit du premier projet à l'échelle du bassin où les pays de l'UE et de l'IPA unissent leurs efforts pour lutter contre les déchets marins par une approche coordonnée, agissant pour (i) diagnostiquer  les impacts des déchets marins » sur la biodiversité dans les AMP et leurs « hots spots , (ii) Définir et tester des mesures de surveillance, prévention et réduction «sur mesure», et (iii) Élaborer une stratégie territoriale commune pour les régions de la zone Interreg en vue de la conservation de la biodiversité. Le plan de gouvernance conjoint, défini par une approche participative, et l'engagement concret des AMPs pour sa mise en œuvre, permettra d'étendre les habitats protégés en vue d'un meilleur état de conservation.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre de plans de gouvernance communs, Surface d'habitats soutenus pour atteindre un meilleur statut de conservation, Nombre de secteurs protégés engagés (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de stratégies de gestion, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

fr.name: Actions conjointes d’efficacité énergétique
fr.description: ENERJ s'appuie sur les leçons apprises par les partenaires lors du soutien, implémentation et suivi des plans et politiques en matière d’efficacité énergétique(EE).Les facteurs affectant la réussite des mesures d'EE sont liés à l’insuffisante intégration des politiques, le manque des ressources humaines adéquates,de stratégies financières efficaces, des mesures spécifiques et des données précises sur la performance énergétique des bâtiments publiques.ENERJ a pour but d’améliorer la réussite des mesures d’EE pour les bâtiment publiques à travers des outils innovants de collaboration pour collecter données,le renforcement de la coopération entre AL pour réaliser actions conjointes à plus grande échelle,l’introduction des formes de financement PP. Les principaux résultats attendus:(i)actions conjointes et intégrées d’EE,à plus grande échelle,capables d’activer économies d’échelle,impacts plus significatifs sur la réduction des émissions,de catalyser une gamme plus large de mécanismes financiers et de tirer profit des Fonds structurels;(ii)développement d’une plateforme web,une géodatabase sur les mesures d’EE adoptées dans le cadre des PAED ou plans énergétiques;(iii)renforcement des capacités des acteurs publics/privés et nomination d’un Coordinateur d'actions conjointes afin d’assurer réalisation et suivi des plans d’EE. L’approche innovante se base sur la réalisation d’actions conjoints, avec le but final d’améliorer l’implémentation des politiques d’EE dans les régions MED.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification disponibles pour le suivi et la gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de stratégies en faveur des plans de gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de groupes cibles participant aux activités de sensibilisation sur l'efficacité énergétique pour les bâtiments publics, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à développer des plans/stratégies d'efficacité énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: Joint Actions for Energy Efficiency
en.description: ENERJ draws on challenges faced by partners in implementing and monitoring EE measures, projects, plans and policies at local level. Common factors that hinder the effectiveness of EE actions are linked to scarce policy integration, inadequacy of qualified human resources, ineffective financial strategies, untailored measures and incomplete information about EE performance of the public building stock. ENERJ increases and improves the efficacy of EE measures for public buildings by using innovative collaborative instruments to gather useful data, fostering cooperation among local authorities on wide-scale Joint actions, promoting public-private ventures. Main outputs will be:(i)integrated large-scale Joint Actions for EE, able to achieve economies of scale, significant impacts on energy consumptions and emissions, catalyze a wider range of investments and leverage Structural Funds through the ROPs;(ii)ENERJ web platform implementation, a geo-database of the EE measures adopted by the municipalities within SEAPs or other local energy plans;(iii)Enhancement of public and private stakeholders skills and delineation of the Joint Actions Coordinator to assess, define, adopt, implement and monitor EE actions and plans.The innovative approach of ENERJ lies in working collectively to propose exchange of experience and cooperation on joint projects, with the final aim to improve the operational aspects of the EE policies and programmes implementation in the Med area.
en.expected_results: 1 Increased number of implemented EE actions and plans, coordinated at district or region level, : ENERJ seeks to increase and improve the effectiveness of EE measures for energy performance in the public building stock by promoting a collaborative, innovative and technologically oriented approach in the definition and deployment of energy-related projects. In order to achieve a more effective implementation of energy actions and plans, the project will foster the collaboration among local authorities and municipalities. Through the introduction of the role of the Coordinator and the creation of an on-line platform, the project will enhance collaboration and achieve common and tailored SEAPs actions. Having experience in energy management, the Coordinator will help local authorities in targeting energy actions to be jointly implemented on the territory. On the other side, the on-line platform will be used as a database to search and gather relevant information about SEAPs, energy plans and EE measures successfully implemented in EU and MED countries. The platform aims to bridge the activity of the Coordinator with the one of Municipalities, facilitating the organization of useful information about existing SEAPs and target energy-related initiatives. The collaborative platform and the role of the coordinator are the two main strategies to achieve the defined results since they will produce tangible benefits in the management of EE measures in public buildings through strengthening knowledge and support the authorities in the decision making and managing process.1 Increased number of implemented EE actions and plans, coordinated at district or region level, : ENERJ seeks to increase and improve the effectiveness of EE measures for energy performance in the public building stock by promoting a collaborative, innovative and technologically oriented approach in the definition and deployment of energy-related projects. In order to achieve a more effective implementation of energy actions and plans, the project will foster the collaboration among local authorities and municipalities. Through the introduction of the role of the Coordinator and the creation of an on-line platform, the project will enhance collaboration and achieve common and tailored SEAPs actions. Having experience in energy management, the Coordinator will help local authorities in targeting energy actions to be jointly implemented on the territory. On the other side, the on-line platform will be used as a database to search and gather relevant information about SEAPs, energy plans and EE measures successfully implemented in EU and MED countries. The platform aims to bridge the activity of the Coordinator with the one of Municipalities, facilitating the organization of useful information about existing SEAPs and target energy-related initiatives. The collaborative platform and the role of the coordinator are the two main strategies to achieve the defined results since they will produce tangible benefits in the management of EE measures in public buildings through strengthening knowledge and support the authorities in the decision making and managing process.2 More significant reduction of energy consumption and GHG emissions due to the Joint Actions. : The defined result will be achieved through a stronger focus on cost-effective structural interventions, which are able to embrace a wider geographical scale (district-level / joint SEAPs), and a more active involvement of citizens and local businesses. The instruments and strategies developed by the project are designed to accomplish this goal and boost the effectiveness of the implementation phase. Through collaborative tools, advisory and capacity building activities, the project will help the public actors and policy makers to address and manage actions and produce real and tangible effects on the reduction of energy consumption in public buildings and GHG emissions. To do so, the figure of the Coordinator and the online data base (containing best practices, successful study cases, etc.) will be crucial to target joint territorial strategies. The platform aims also to be a “meeting place” of local stakeholder that is potentially involved in the actions and projects, enabling to foster opportunities for public and private cooperation (private investments, public tenders and introduction of new financial model for energy refurbishment, such as ESCO). Thanks to this approach, the project expects to optimize the application of energy plans and adapted measures for public buildings that will consequently bring to reach the expected energy performance targets and the overall reduction of GHG emissions.3 More effective implementation of policy instruments and improvement of access to funding methods through public-private joint ventures. : ENERJ project aims at improving the effectiveness of energy-related policy instruments and plans (national energy plans and SEAPs) by studying and promoting new financial mechanism (EPC, green taxation, public-private joint ventures, etc.) for EE measures for the public building stock. The defined result will be achieved mainly through activities that are addressed to local authorities and are focused on: raise awareness, capacity building , coordination in management of joint actions, integration of innovative financial schemes, advisory on drafting of plans and joint actions. Thanks to the activity of the Joint Actions Coordinator promoted by the project, the municipalities will be supported in the definition and coordination of integrated joint EE interventions on public building stock. Besides this, it will be also studied how these interventions can be backed through innovative forms of financing, such as public-private joint ventures, which can boost the energy upgrading process. As an additional support to this, the project’s online database and platform will help in the decision making and guidance at governance level. The platform contains a collection of good practices, energy plans and policies, guidance on new financial mechanism and will allow a better visibility of the investment financing opportunities for private stakeholders (tenders, EPC, etc.), thus strengthening the collaboration with private companies that are willing to invest in this sector.
en.achievements: During the specific period from 1/1/2019 - 30/6/2019 the ENERJ project has focused on the finalization of the Joint Action Plans of each partner as this is the main output of the project as well as on finally elaborating the online web platform which has caused delay to the project as a whole in the first place. Partners now had the opportunity to upload the data gathered in previous semesters regarding public buildings energy audits.In addition, the project has continued to disseminate the results through the organization of local conferences in partners' areas and the preparation of the respective newsletter. The project during this period has participated in several external events like the International Conference in Athens - VERDE TEC, the National Conference in Crete within the framework of the IMPULSE - Interreg MED Project and the SBE19 Sustainable Built Environment International Conference in Scilla, Italy . Finally, the 6th Steering Committee - Transnational meeting was organized in Nicosia, Cyprus with the participation and contribution of project partners.
en.expected_outputs: Joint Actions Strategy,ENERJ Web platform,Methodology for implementing the Joint Actions
en.delivered_outputs: Number of available planning tools to monitor and manage energy consumption in public buildings, Number of strategies supporting plans on energy consumption management for public buildings, Number of target groups participating in capacity raising activities on energy efficiency for public buildings, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in developing energy efficiency plans/strategies, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 2,

fr.name: Des stratégies touristiques alternatives pour améliorer le développement durable du tourisme par la promotion de l'identité méditerranéenenne
fr.description: La Méditerranée est menacée à cause de la pratique inappropriée et le développement associé au tourisme en masse. En conséquence, les zones de forte attraction touristique dans les villes côtières sont en train d’atteindre leur capacité de charge, en ayant un impact direct sur l’environnement urbain ainsi que sur des éléments qui sont définis par la Culture MED. En ce contexte, ALTER ECO met en valeur le développement durable du tourisme en promouvant l’Identité Méditerranée à travers la mise en œuvre de stratégies touristiques alternatives dans 6 pilotes co-désignés et mis en œuvre par les acteurs publics et privés. Le projet fournit l’opportunité de tester, dans les villes représentatives MED qui sont utilisées comme Laboratoire Vivant, les méthodologies et les outils qui ont surgi dans projets à impact élevé précédents dans le domaine du tourisme durable ou proposées par les acteurs principaux, avec l’objectif de parvenir à des stratégies touristiques 	holistiques et réalistes au niveau local et régional qui permettent la transférabilité sur le territoire MED. Les résultats du projet soutiendront les décideurs à prendre des décisions plus éclairées et intégrées pour la gouvernance et la gestion du tourisme dans la Méditerranée, et en même temps, ils permettront d'améliorer la coordination des actions entre les acteurs publics et privés en faveur de la mise en œuvre des stratégies soulevées afin de créer de nouvelles opportunités commerciales.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'instruments disponibles pour améliorer le développement de tourisme durable et responsable, Nombre de destinations touristiques couvertes par un outil d'évaluation de tourisme durable, Nombre de stratégies appliquant des critères de gestion de tourisme durables, Nombre de régions et des sous-régions engagées (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de plans de tourisme durables, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

en.name: Alternative tourist strategies to enhance the local sustainable development of tourism by promoting Mediterranean Identity
en.description:
The Mediterranean is under threat due to the inappropriate practice and development associated with mass tourism.As a consequence areas of high tourist attraction in coastal cities are reaching their limit on carrying capacity, with a direct impact not only on the urban environment but on key elements that define MED Culture.In this context, ALTER ECO enhances the local sustainable development of tourism by promoting Mediterranean Identity through the implementation of alternative tourist strategies in 6 pilots co-designed and implemented by public and private stakeholders. The project provides the opportunity of testing, in representative MED cities, used as LIVING LAB, existing methodologies and tools arisen in previous high impact projects in the field of sustainable tourism or proposed by key stakeholders, with the aim of reaching holistic and realistic tourist strategies at local and regional level that allow transferability in the MED territory. Project results will support policy makers to make more informed and integrated decisions for the governance and management of tourism in the Mediterranean and at the same time will enhance the coordination of actions between public and private stakeholders towards the implementation of the raised strategies in order to create new business opportunities.
ALTER ECO will contribute to improve knowledge and decision making capacities, including a better use of observation, monitoring and planning in the field of sustainable tourism.
en.expected_results: 1 Reduction and better manage the impact of tourism activities on the environment : The Mediterranean Sea is one of the most diverse marine environments in the world and is recognized as one of the world’s 25 hotspots for biodiversity (UNEP, 2010). The Mediterranean is under threat due to the inappropriate practice and development associated with mass tourism. With current development models based on quantity, the projected growth of tourism development in the region will continue to damage landscapes, cause soil erosion, put pressure on endangered species, further strain available water resources, increase waste and pollution discharges into the sea, lead to cultural disruption and urbanization, artificialization of coastal zones and natural areas. Historical centres and other places of high tourist attraction in the Med coastal cities are being transformed into monofuncional cities with low density, no proximity to basic services, acoustic and waste pollution and a hard risk of reaching the limit of their charging capacity. ALTER ECO aims to reduce and better manage the impact of tourism activities on the environment (adaptation of activities, seasonality, land use, water management, energy management, etc.) through the decrease of the concentration of human activities in hotspots where carrying capacity is exceeded. This result will be reached through the diversification of the offer, considering environmental, cultural and economic impact.This result places special emphasis on the environmental component of sustainable tourism1 Reduction and better manage the impact of tourism activities on the environment : The Mediterranean Sea is one of the most diverse marine environments in the world and is recognized as one of the world’s 25 hotspots for biodiversity (UNEP, 2010). The Mediterranean is under threat due to the inappropriate practice and development associated with mass tourism. With current development models based on quantity, the projected growth of tourism development in the region will continue to damage landscapes, cause soil erosion, put pressure on endangered species, further strain available water resources, increase waste and pollution discharges into the sea, lead to cultural disruption and urbanization, artificialization of coastal zones and natural areas. Historical centres and other places of high tourist attraction in the Med coastal cities are being transformed into monofuncional cities with low density, no proximity to basic services, acoustic and waste pollution and a hard risk of reaching the limit of their charging capacity. ALTER ECO aims to reduce and better manage the impact of tourism activities on the environment (adaptation of activities, seasonality, land use, water management, energy management, etc.) through the decrease of the concentration of human activities in hotspots where carrying capacity is exceeded. This result will be reached through the diversification of the offer, considering environmental, cultural and economic impact.This result places special emphasis on the environmental component of sustainable tourism2 Improvement of the social sustainability of MED tourist destinations : Mediterranean space combines many appealing features (climate, coastlines, etc.) and is one of the cradles of civilization with a rich natural and cultural heritage. Thus, it is the most popular tourist region in Europe and one of the largest tourist areas in the world. However, it is under threat due to the inappropriate practice and development associated with mass tourism. As a consequence, historical centres and other places of high tourist attraction in Med coastal cities, are reaching their limit on carrying capacity, with a direct impact not only on the urban environment but on key elements that define MED Culture: classical city configuration and the “way of life”. Med Cultural identity is being lost creating “scenarios” that could be placed in any other place in the world. Additionally, international firms are gradually displacing the traditional local businesses, with the consequences in unemployment. This loose of complexity of uses and functions is transforming these areas in “theme parks” where the residential use is drastically reduced, together with the small and traditional business. ALTER ECO will improve the social sustainability of MED tourist destinations by highlighting the value of MED tourist destinations. This result will be reached by reinforcing Mediterranean identity and visibility and through the promotion of local tradition and common cultural heritage.This result places special emphasis on the social component of sustainable tourism3 Improving cooperation, joint and integrated planning and better manage conflicting interests : Tourism industry is a complex adaptive system involving many independent actors. The adoption of sustainable tourism practices does not only mean the destination level implementation but also the adoption of a set practices by every actor involved. Therefore, one of the main challenges of the project is to improve cooperation, joint and integrated planning and to better manage conflicting interests to enhance the development of tourism as an asset for the sustainability of MED regions. This result will be achieved by proposing alternative strategies to help tourist destinations to improve their cooperation strategies and policies and joint planning for the development of a more sustainable and responsible coastal tourism. Strategies reported in D3.4.3, will include alternative tourist tactics to improve ICZM, to adapt public policies, economic activities and planning tools and to enhance a better cooperation between stakeholders. In addition, D3.1.1, including latest trends in methodologies and tools addressed to raise the level of sustainability of tourist destinations, will help to remove barriers to technological innovation to ease the implementation of sustainable tourism measures. A special emphasis will be placed to enhance the coordination of actions between public and private stakeholders towards the implementation of the raised strategies. This result places special emphasis on the economic component of sustainable tourism
en.achievements: ALTER ECO is working on improving the balance between tourist attraction as a source of economic growth and the preservation of the classic values of the Mediterranean city as an example of sustainability, through the implementation of alternative tourist strategies in six pilots by the interested agents.Venice, Dubrovnik, Rhodes, Genoa, Comunitat Valenciana, and Málaga worked on reducing the concentration of human activities in hotspots, highlighting the value of MED identity and creating new business opportunities through a living lab methodology that tries to involve all the stakeholders. The partners have adapted the project philosophy depending on the diagnosis carried out of each destination, resulting in a great diversity of solutions proposed that will be easily transferred to other MED regions with similar characteristics. Partners learned from each other’s previous experience, working together to obtain better results, as for example in the field of technologies used in people flows monitoring and regarding possible methods to promote and protect local and Mediterranean identity. Until now, on the one hand, ALTER ECO consortium identified the most representative Mediterranean identity characteristics and the level of threat they face due to uncontrolled tourism activities and proposed a method for calculating the carrying capacity limit (CCL) in order to be able to link the threats the cities face with their proximity to the CCL. On the other hand, pilots implemented 15 measures in 7 destinations resulting in transferable actions that will be capitalized during the last phase of the project. The ultimate objective is to provide a validated methodological framework for the estimation of the CCL in touristic destinations and its relationship with the loss of the Mediterranean identity, in order to make better-informed decisions and provide strategic insights towards the proposal effective solutions.
en.expected_outputs: Preliminary studies: common methodologies,Pilot activities,Design of innovative tourism strategies,Model for implementation of innovative tourism strategies,ALTER ECO Declaration,Interregional learning Portfolio backed by Good Practices Database,Proposals-Recommendations for application of the Model in the Med area
en.delivered_outputs: Number of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism, Number of tourist destinations covered by a sustainable tourism evaluation tool, Number of strategies applying sustainable tourism management criteria, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in implementing sustainable tourism plans, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 3,

fr.name: Croissance verte grâce à la capitalisation des serres innovantes
fr.description:
Le projet valorisera principalement les résultats du projet LIFE+"Adapt2change" par la promotion, la diffusion d'information et la mise en œuvre de serres innovantes dans la zone MED pour minimiser la demande en eau & en énergie. Le projet renforcera la sensibilisation environnementale liée à l'efficience énergétique et hydrique et à la production durable, contribuant à la croissance verte et au développement durable. Les résultats attendus:
- Créer un cluster / réseau agricole transnational innovant par un protocole d'accord.
- Élaborer des recommandations politiques favorisant la collaboration entre les acteurs de la quadruple hélice et améliorer le cadre existant des investissements éco-innovants.
-Transférer les connaissances acquises par un plan d'action MED conjoint et une plate-forme e-learning, supports de formation, ateliers et webinaires.
- Identifier des possibilités financières pour répondre aux besoins de chaque région.
L'objectif global: améliorer les capacités d'éco-innovation des acteurs publics et privés dans le secteur de la production agricole sous serres par le renforcement de la coopération transnationale, le transfert des connaissances et le développement de réseaux de collaboration. Les bénéficiaires seront: Producteurs de cultures sous serres, industrie agroalimentaire, décideurs, syndicats des coopératives agricoles et instituts de recherche et technologie. Ils seront invités aux évènements du projet et contribueront à la réalisation de ses objectifs.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d’instruments opérationnels en faveur de l’innovation des PME, Nombre d'entreprises béneficiant de subventions, Nombre d'entreprises recevant un soutien non-financier, Nombre de clusters d'innovation transnationaux soutenus, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 1,

en.name: Green Growth through the capitalization of innovative Greenhouses
en.description:
The project will mainly capitalize results of LIFE+ "Adapt2change" project by promoting, disseminating & transferring innovative Greenhouses in the MED area, minimizing water & energy demand. The project will stimulate environmental awareness on issues related to energy & water efficiency & sustainable production, contributing to Green Growth & promoting sustainable development. Particularly, the partners will (main outputs): 
- Create a transnational innovative cluster / agricultural network through a memorandum of agreement.
- Develop tailored policy recommendations favoring collaboration between the actors of quadruple helix, enhancing existing framework for eco-innovation investments.
- Transfer existing knowledge gained through the elaboration of a joint MED Action Plan & e-learning platform/training course material/workshops/webinars.
- Identify possible financial opportunities formulated to the needs of each region.
The overall objective is to Improve eco-innovation capacities of public & private actors in the greenhouse/agriculture sector through stronger transnational cooperation, knowledge transfer & better collaborative networks. Main beneficiaries will be Greenhouse Farmers, Businesses specialized in Agro-food & Greenhouse industry, Policy Makers - Unions of Agricultural Cooperatives, Research Technology Institutes who will be invited to participate in seminars, workshops, consultations & the Final Conference, contributing to the achievement of project objectives.
en.expected_results: 1 Develop, promote & integrate policy Recommendations in local & regional planning in order to boost eco-innovative investments at transnational : In order to increase the transnational activity of innovative clusters and networks of Agriculture sector in the MED area, the project consortium will systematize the existing knowledge assessing strategies and policies at transnational level as well available financial channels of innovative investments. Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities and Threats will be recorded and assessed (including a gap-analysis) in order to develop tailored policy recommendations favouring eco-innovation of SMEs, improving eco-innovation capacity of policy makers and enhancing existing models of cooperation between quadruple helix or developing new ones.1 Develop, promote & integrate policy Recommendations in local & regional planning in order to boost eco-innovative investments at transnational : In order to increase the transnational activity of innovative clusters and networks of Agriculture sector in the MED area, the project consortium will systematize the existing knowledge assessing strategies and policies at transnational level as well available financial channels of innovative investments. Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities and Threats will be recorded and assessed (including a gap-analysis) in order to develop tailored policy recommendations favouring eco-innovation of SMEs, improving eco-innovation capacity of policy makers and enhancing existing models of cooperation between quadruple helix or developing new ones.2 Reinforce Agricultural Innovative Cluster in the MED area creating synergies & cooperation mechanisms between the actors of quadruple helix : The project aims at developing synergies and cooperation mechanisms between the actors of quadruple helix (research bodies, businesses, including agricultural associations, public authorities and civil societies) in order to strengthen transnational agricultural innovative clusters. A first vital step will be the developement of a Memorandum of Agreement/Understanding between relevant actors in order to formalise a group of actors in a joint line sharing the common needs and targets. The project has the capacity to achieve this goal as it has directly (partners) and indirectly (associated beneficiaries) engaged competent bodies that have the political-institutional (e.g. regional bodies included), technical (Agricultural Univiersities and Instititutes) and bodies that facilitate the wider dissemination and engagement of the public (e.g. Development Agencies).3 Increase capacity building of innovative clusters and networks through knowledge transfer and training programmes : The capacity building of actors will be improved through activities of knowledge transferring. In particular, a training scheme and e-learning platform (including events, courses, webinars, workshops) will be deployed and training course material will be designed and produced in order to promote and transfer the existing knowledge (geothermal greenhouse installations) to stakeholders and actors of quadruple helix. The selected partnerships, through the robust methods for capacity building that are employed, is considered optimum for effective policy making, training and capacity building.

fr.name: Réseaux du programme MED pour une coopération innovante en matière d'efficacité énergétique
fr.description: En tant que plateforme de solutions innovantes et partagées d’efficacité énergétique, le projet MEDNICE ancrera une communauté MED autour des questions liées à l’énergie qui se posent aux organismes publics, afin de démultiplier les résultats des projets modulaires et leur impact sur les politiques publiques. En région MED, la conception d’une majorité des bâtiments publics ne permet d'améliorer leur performance en matière d’efficacité énergétique (EE). Ceci est dû en partie à un manque de connaissances des réponses communes à ce défi transnational. L’objectif de MEDNICE est donc de renforcer les capacités des partenaires des projets MED à travers la mise en place d’une communauté MED et d’un cadre transnational centrés sur l’EE dans les bâtiments publics. En instaurant des mécanismes de formation et d’apprentissage mutuel, MEDNICE contribuera à renforcer les capacités des gestionnaires du patrimoine bâti afin de développer et mettre en œuvre de meilleures pratiques d’EE. Une plateforme de connaissances permettra de capitaliser et communiquer les résultats des projets. Pierre angulaire du réseau transnational de pairs, elle posera les bases d’une identité MED. MEDNICE synthétisera et analysera les pratiques d’EE via des notes thématiques et politiques à promouvoir au bénéfice de la communauté MED. En s’appuyant sur des ambassadeurs, il disséminera et transférera les messages pour plaider pour des politiques EE spécifiques MED et les intégrer dans les stratégies appropriées.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification disponibles pour le suivi et la gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de stratégies en faveur des plans de gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de groupes cibles participant aux activités de sensibilisation sur l'efficacité énergétique pour les bâtiments publics, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à développer des plans/stratégies d'efficacité énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: MED programme Networks for an Innovative Cooperation in Energy efficiency
en.description: As a MED hub for Energy Efficiency (EE) innovative and shared solutions, the MEDNICE project will anchor a MED community around energy issues that public organisations face in order to promote modular projects’ results and increase their impact on public policies. Indeed, in the MED area, the majority of public buildings is not adequately designed to reduce their energy consumption and improve their EE performance. This is partly due to a lack of awareness of owners and managers and knowledge gaps regarding common answers to this transnational challenge. Thus the overall objective of MEDNICE is to empower MED projects' partners through the establishment of a MED community and a joint transnational framework around energy efficiency in public buildings. By setting up training and peer learning mechanisms, MEDNICE will contribute to increase the capacity of owners and managers of public buildings to design and implement better energy efficiency practices. It will set up a knowledge platform that will allow for capitalizing and communicating results and outputs of the modular projects. It will serve as a cornerstone of a transnational peer network laying the ground for a MED identity. MEDNICE will also analyze and summarize EE practices through thematic and policy papers to be promoted and benefit the MED community. Relying on Ambassadors this will help disseminate and transfer messages in order to advocate for specific MED EE policies and mainstream them in relevant strategies.
en.expected_results: 1 Policy harmonization : MEDNICE will enable to achieve a greater public policy harmonization regarding the energy efficiency in public buildings issues specific to the Mediterranean. Indeed, the white papers , developed according to the EEB Hub's recommendations, following the modular projects results' systematization, will be used to develop EU and regional policy papers enabling proper, efficient and fruitful EU and regional advocacy campaigns. With these actions, it will facilitate the policy harmonization in the countries of the northern shore of the Mediterranean.1 Policy harmonization : MEDNICE will enable to achieve a greater public policy harmonization regarding the energy efficiency in public buildings issues specific to the Mediterranean. Indeed, the white papers , developed according to the EEB Hub's recommendations, following the modular projects results' systematization, will be used to develop EU and regional policy papers enabling proper, efficient and fruitful EU and regional advocacy campaigns. With these actions, it will facilitate the policy harmonization in the countries of the northern shore of the Mediterranean.
en.achievements: During the fourth reporting period, the MEDNICE partnership has undertaken these tasks :- A smooth day-to-day management through regular e-mails, conference calls, and steering committees, and the "quality assurance" activity. ALBEA, the external contractor in charge of the quality assurance has shown in its last report improvements in the management of the project and the realisation of activities. - The third series of teleconferences, so called Regular Contact (RC) calls with the 10 Modular Projects (MPs) in the first week of October in order to be updated about the progress of each project and boost the interaction between MPs and HPs. The focus was on the contribution of MPs to policy papers and advocacy work. It was also an excellent opportunity to prepare the contributions of MPs to the 2 annual MEDNICE conference, in particular to the exhibition and the training workshops. - The organisation of the Efficient Building and Renewable Energy MED Community Conference (Ljubljana, Slovenia) (2nd annual conference), with the participation of the DG ENER, in collaboration with GREENCAP (RES community).- The mobilization of IPA Associate partners and the development of activities for the establishment of IPA Community.- The MEDNICE Technical Papers and Lessons Learned Reports. According to the main results of the MPs, the main topics of the deliverables to be developed in 2018 and 2019 have been defined. For this year, there will be two Technical Papers: “MED Financing schemes and barriers” and “Tools, methodologies and indicators developed in MED”. - The participated in external events of the MED Programme, in order to promote the work done in the framework of the EB Communiyt- The participation in the Euro-Mediterranean Platform on Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energyset up by the European Commission and developed by the Union for the Mediterranean. It has been proposed to MEDNICE to become an actor of this platform.- The preparation of the first policy papers.- The establishement of first contacts with partner organisation in Spain and Greece in order to carry out 4 national advocacy/policy debates on EE in public buildings. The idea is to organise workshops of 2-3 hours each involving around 30 influencers such as interest groups or multiplier organisations, local/regional entities from the Modular Projects knowing the needs on the ground as well as policy-makers from the respective country.
en.expected_outputs: Energy Efficiency in public Building Taskforce (EEB Taskforce),Energy Efficiency in public Building Hub (EEB Hub)
en.delivered_outputs: Number of available planning tools to monitor and manage energy consumption in public buildings, Number of strategies supporting plans on energy consumption management for public buildings, Number of target groups participating in capacity raising activities on energy efficiency for public buildings, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in developing energy efficiency plans/strategies, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 2,
